tool.name: TOGA2 ortholog rescue
tool.description: project reference gene models onto a query genome via chain alignment + CESAR2 (WF-C.4 pass 2)
tool.command:
## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ## WF-C.4 TOGA2/CESAR2 rescue. ## ## TOGA2 (toga2.py, v2.0.x) replaces the TOGA1 `toga REF.bed12 CHAIN ## REF.fa QUERY.fa --pn ... --cb ... --u12 "" --kt --filter_bed ... --nc` ## interface with a click subcommand: `toga2.py run`. Differences: ## * genomes are passed as .2bit (--ref_2bit/--query_2bit), NOT FASTA; ## we convert the staged soft-masked FASTAs with the bundled ## faToTwoBit (on PATH inside /opt/TOGA2/bin). ## * isoforms -> --isoform_file (was --cb) ## * "no U12 table" -> --no_u12_file flag (was --u12 "") ## * no SpliceAI predictions staged -> --no_spliceai ## * --keep_temporary_files replaces --kt ## * --parallel_strategy local runs Nextflow over local CPUs (no SLURM) ## * TOGA2 `run` has NO --filter_bed; projection scope is driven by the ## chains + reference annotation. The Phase C.2 triage BED is therefore ## no longer a TOGA input (the upstream driver gate still decides ## whether to invoke this tool at all). ## ## Galaxy stores datasets as extensionless .dat; stage real-named ## symlinks so faToTwoBit / TOGA2 see proper FASTA/BED/chain names. ## ------------------------------------------------------------------ ln -s '$ref_bed12' ref.bed12 && ln -s '$chain' cleaned.chain && ln -s '$ref_fasta' ref.fa && ln -s '$query_fasta' query.fa && ln -s '$isoforms' isoforms.tsv &&
## FASTA -> 2bit (TOGA2 indexes 2bit, not FASTA) faToTwoBit ref.fa ref.2bit && faToTwoBit query.fa query.2bit &&
toga2.py run --ref_2bit ref.2bit --query_2bit query.2bit --chain_file cleaned.chain --ref_annotation ref.bed12 --isoform_file isoforms.tsv --no_u12_file --no_spliceai --parallel_strategy local --feature_jobs \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} --orthology_jobs \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} --tree_cpus \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} --keep_temporary_files --output toga_out

param.name: ref_bed12
param.label: Reference annotation (BED12)
param.help: Reference gene models in BED12, one transcript per line (--ref_annotation). Column 4 transcript ids must match the --isoform_file table.

param.name: chain
param.label: Reference-to-query chain
param.help: Cleaned UCSC chain alignment of reference onto query (--chain_file), e.g. {A}.{Q}.cleaned.chain from Phase C. TOGA2 derives projection loci from these chains.

param.name: ref_fasta
param.label: Reference genome (soft-masked)
param.help: Soft-masked reference assembly FASTA. Converted to 2bit (--ref_2bit) with the bundled faToTwoBit. Seqids must match the BED/chain reference names.

param.name: query_fasta
param.label: Query genome (soft-masked)
param.help: Soft-masked query assembly FASTA. Converted to 2bit (--query_2bit). CESAR2 re-aligns flagged exons against this.

param.name: isoforms
param.label: Isoforms table
param.help: Two-column tab-separated gene/transcript table (--isoform_file): gene_id <tab> transcript_id, grouping the BED12 transcripts into genes.

tool.help:
**TOGA2 ortholog rescue (WF-C.4 pass 2)**
Container-only wrapper of `hillerlab/TOGA2`. Projects reference gene models onto a query genome through a cleaned chain alignment and re-aligns triage-flagged exons with CESAR2, producing the fallback annotation that Phase C.4 merge folds in alongside the accepted Liftoff projection.
Faithful port (re-mapped to the TOGA2 CLI) of the `toga ...` invocation in `Pv4-pangenome/v3/pipeline/impl/04_annotate_project.sh`.
-----
**Inputs**
- Reference annotation (BED12) -> `--ref_annotation` - Reference -> query chain -> `--chain_file` - Reference genome (soft-masked FASTA, converted to 2bit) -> `--ref_2bit` - Query genome (soft-masked FASTA, converted to 2bit) -> `--query_2bit` - Isoforms table -> `--isoform_file`
Fixed flags: `--no_u12_file`, `--no_spliceai`, `--parallel_strategy local`, `--keep_temporary_files`, `--feature_jobs/--orthology_jobs/--tree_cpus ${GALAXY_SLOTS}`, `--output toga_out`.
**Outputs** (all four consumed by Phase C.4 `phase_c4_merge.py --toga-dir`)
- `query_annotation.bed` - projected query transcript models - `query_genes.bed` - projected query gene-level models - `orthology_classification.tsv` - per-projection orthology call - `loss_summary.tsv` - intactness / gene-loss status per projection
-----
**TOGA1 -> TOGA2 CLI migration note**
This wrapper targets **TOGA2** (`toga2.py run`, v2.0.x), which restructured the TOGA1 (`toga.py`, v1.1.7) interface (TOGA1 -> TOGA2):
- 4 positional args (bed, chain, ref.fa, query.fa) -> `--ref_annotation --chain_file --ref_2bit --query_2bit` (2bit, not FASTA) - `--cb isoforms.tsv` -> `--isoform_file isoforms.tsv` - `--u12 ""` -> `--no_u12_file` - (new) -> `--no_spliceai` - `--kt` -> `--keep_temporary_files` - `--nc N` -> `--feature_jobs/--orthology_jobs/--tree_cpus N` plus `--parallel_strategy local` - `--pn DIR` -> `--output DIR` - `--filter_bed needs_cesar2.bed` -> removed; no per-gene filter in `run`
-----
**Operational notes**
- *Container.* `toga2:local` is built from the upstream apptainer.def, translated to a Dockerfile (`FROM continuumio/anaconda3`). The image has no published Docker tag upstream; rebuild locally or pin to a digest once published. - *Gating.* The upstream driver skips this step when the chain is missing or the Phase C.2 rescue set is empty. - *Scale.* TOGA2 runs Nextflow over real genome alignments; it is not expected to complete on toy inputs and is IO/CPU heavy.
